MINI MINUTES FOR OCT-15-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LIBERT MYERS' MOTION FOR INJUNCTIVE AND DECLATORY RELIEF IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 14, 2014 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN OCTOBER 15, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS. MR. WOODS STATED ON THE RECORD THAT HE SHALL PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO THE COURT TODAY. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER CSR# 5976. (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT LIBERT MYERS' MOTION FOR INJUNCTIVE AND DECLARATORY RELIEF RELEASING PROPERTY OF ERRONEOUSLY IDENTIFIED OWNER IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 697.410(C). THE MOVING PARTY (LIBERT MYERS SR.) ESTABLISHES THAT HE IS NOT THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR AND THE JUDGMENT WAS ENTERED AGAINST HIS SON (LIBERT MYERS JR.). THE COLLECTION EFFORTS AGAINST THE FATHER ARE IMPROPER AND HE IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF. PLAINTIFF SHALL PREPARE AND DELIVER A RECORDABLE DOCUMENT RELEASING THE LIENS TO MOVING PARTY'S ATTORNEY WITHIN TEN DAYS FROM ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; SHERYL L. SAWYER CSR# 5976. (302)

calendar.matters:
Mtn For Injunctive And Declatory Relief; Notice
Off Calendar Nov-14-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Michael F. Woods, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael Woods, Tel. 415.759.1900, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The matter was discussed. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT LIBERT MYERS' Motion For Injunctive And Declaratory Relief Releasing Property of Erroneously Identified Owner is granted pursuant to CCP 697.410(c). The moving party (Libert Myers Sr.) establishes that he is not the judgment debtor and the judgment was entered against his son (Libert Myers Jr.). The collection efforts against the father are improper and he is entitled to relief. Plaintiff shall prepare and deliver a recordable document releasing the liens to moving party's attorney within ten days from entry of this order. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Mtn For Injunctive And Declatory Relief; Notice
Off Calendar Oct-15-2014 Continued To Nov-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael F. Woods, Esq. of the Law Office of Michael F. Woods, T:415.759.1900, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. Defendant Libert Myers' Motion For Injunctive And Declatory Relief is continued to November 14, 2014 at 9:30 am in Dept. 302 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than October 15, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Mr. Woods stated on the record that he shall provide courtesy copies of the moving papers to the court today. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer CSR# 5976.
